name: Korean Home-Style Scallion Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon cold water, or enough to make moderately thin batter (about 2 ounces)
1/2 cup chopped sweet red or green peppers (~ cut into 2-inch lengths)
1/4 teaspoon dried hot red chili flakes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine flour and salt with cold water until a moderately thin batter forms.
2. Add ground beef, chopped peppers, and scallions to the batter; mix gently but thoroughly.
3. Heat an 8-inch skillet over medium-high heat and add corn oil.
4. Pour in enough batter to form a pancake about 1/4 inch thick.
5. Fry for 3 to 4 minutes on one side, then carefully flip and fry another 3 to 4 minutes until cooked through.
6. Remove the pancake from the skillet and keep warm.
7. Add more oil if needed, and cook the remaining batter in the same manner.
8. Transfer pancakes to a cutting board; cut each into 2-inch pieces.
9. Arrange the pieces back into their original pancake shapes or serve them uncut.
10. Serve hot with Dipping Sauce.
DIPPING SAUCE:
Combine all ingredients in a small bowl and serve at the table with the pancakes. Makes about 3 tablespoons.
